scope: Refactor 4 hook files to fix CQRS violations by using hivemind_journal tool instead of direct writer calls

blocked_routes.reason: client.tool.call() does NOT exist in the current SDK (@opencode-ai/sdk@^1.2.27). The client.tool namespace only has ids() and list() methods.
blocked_routes.evidence: src/tools/hivemind-journal.ts defines the tool with execute(), but hooks do not have ToolContext to invoke it directly

blocked_routes.reason: hivemind_journal tool only supports event types: assistant_output, user_message, tool_call, compaction, trajectory, diagnostic. The session_meta event type (needed for session.json writes) is NOT supported.
blocked_routes.evidence: hivemind-journal.ts line 28-34 defines JournalEventType without session_meta

blocked_routes.reason: createRecoveryCheckpoint writes to trajectory-ledger.json via createTrajectoryCheckpoint(). This is NOT the same as a trajectory event - it's a checkpoint operation. Not supported by hivemind_journal tool.
blocked_routes.evidence: src/recovery/recovery-engine.ts line 96-101 calls createTrajectoryCheckpoint, not appendSessionEvent

appendSessionEvent.limitation: Cannot be invoked from hooks - tool.execute() requires ToolContext

recordTrajectoryEvent.limitation: Cannot be invoked from hooks - tool.execute() requires ToolContext

appendSessionDiagnostic.limitation: Cannot be invoked from hooks - tool.execute() requires ToolContext

initOrUpdateSessionMetadata.limitation: Writes to session.json - not handled by hivemind_journal tool

createRecoveryCheckpoint.limitation: Writes checkpoint to trajectory-ledger.json - different from trajectory events

sdk_analysis.evidence: OpencodeClient.tool only exposes ids() and list() methods (sdk.gen.d.ts line 78-87). No call() method exists.
sdk_analysis.session_prompt_considered: session.prompt() is designed for conversational interaction, not tool invocation. It sends messages to LLM, not tool execution calls.

architectural_gap: Hooks cannot invoke hivemind_journal tool because: (1) tool.execute() requires ToolContext which hooks don't have, (2) client.tool.call() doesn't exist in SDK, (3) session.prompt() is not for tool invocation
recommendations: Option 1: Add client.tool.call() to SDK - would allow hooks to invoke tools directly
Option 2: Create internal writer API - hooks call internal functions that wrap tool logic, but this doesn't actually route through the tool
Option 3: Accept direct writer calls from hooks - hooks are part of the plugin, not agents, so CQRS may not apply in the same way
Option 4: Separate hook-specific writer functions that hivemind_journal tool delegates to

verification_commands_ran.grep_writer_calls: 16 matches found - refactor not yet applied
